name: Canet de Mar - Sant Pol de Mar - Arenys de Munt Loop
name_original: Desde Carbonella rodeando Arenys de Mar – circular cerca del mar
description: This easy 15.0-mile road cycling loop near Canet de Mar offers coastal views and quiet roads through Sant Pol de Mar and Arenys de Munt.
region_intro: Road cycling around Canet De Mar offers a blend of coastal and mountainous terrain. The region features the Mediterranean coastline, providing routes with sea views, and extends inland into rolling hills and natural parks. Cyclists can navigate well-paved surfaces, with some segments potentially unpaved, through areas like Montnegre y el Corredor and Montseny Natural Park. This diverse landscape provides varied elevation profiles, from gentle coastal loops to challenging climbs. Best road cycling routes around Canet De Mar The most popular road cycling route is Canet de Mar - Sant Pol de Mar - Arenys de Munt Loop, a 15.0 miles (24.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and connects several towns. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Arenys de Mar towards Arenys de Munt - circular via the seafront promenade, a moderate 13.0 miles (20.9 km) path. It follows the seafront promenade, providing scenic coastal scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Sant Pol de Mar – Canet de Mar Beach loop from Canet de Mar, a 36.0 miles (57.9 km) trail leading through the Mediterranean coastline, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Canet De Mar is defined by the Mediterranean coastline, rolling hills, and access to natural parks like Montnegre y el Corredor. The network offers options for easy coastal rides as well as more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Canet De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 7,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Canet De Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Canet de Mar?
A: Canet de Mar offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 40 distinct tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging climbs into the natural parks.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Canet de Mar?
A: Yes, Canet de Mar has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000912410/canet-de-mar-sant-pol-de-mar-arenys-de-munt-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canet de Mar - Sant Pol de Mar - Arenys de Munt Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 24.2 km trail that offers pleasant coastal views and connects several towns.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes near Canet de Mar?
A: You can expect a diverse range of scenery, blending the Mediterranean coastline with rolling hills and natural parks. Routes often feature stunning sea views, lush woodlands in areas like Montnegre y el Corredor, and expansive natural beauty when venturing towards Montseny Natural Park.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in the area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes extend into mountainous terrain with considerable elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18234722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canet de Mar Beach – Montseny Forest Road loop from Arenys de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 97.4 km with nearly 1,860m of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21225177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordoriol Pass – Revell Pass loop from Arenys de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, featuring over 2,200m of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Canet de Mar?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Canet de Mar are designed as loops. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39551142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pol de Mar – Canet de Mar Beach loop from Canet de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you along the Mediterranean coastline for 57.9 km.

Q: What natural parks are accessible by road bike from Canet de Mar?
A: Canet de Mar provides excellent access to natural parks. You can cycle into the Montnegre y el Corredor Natural Park, known for its dense forests, or embark on longer, more challenging rides into the extensive Montseny Natural Park, which offers significant elevation changes and diverse ecosystems.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or cultural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can encounter cultural and historical points of interest. Within Montnegre y el Corredor, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1452213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corredor Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious site. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4574339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupit Cross&lt;/a&gt;, offering historical views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1015284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calella Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; on coastal rides.

Q: Are there routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the region features several spots with rewarding panoramic views. Locations like the Pedracastell Cross (Creu de Pedracastell) offer sweeping vistas of the Mediterranean coast and surrounding hills, providing excellent photo opportunities and a chance to appreciate the scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Canet de Mar?
A: The road cycling routes around Canet de Mar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain that combines stunning coastal views with challenging climbs into natural parks, as well as the well-paved surfaces.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Canet de Mar?
A: The Maresme coast generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, while even winter can be suitable for riding, offering milder weather compared to many other regions.

Q: How long are the road cycling routes typically around Canet de Mar?
A: Route lengths vary significantly to suit different preferences. You can find shorter loops, such as the 20.9 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995678766/arenys-de-mar-towards-arenys-de-munt-circular-via-the-seafront-promenade" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenys de Mar towards Arenys de Munt - circular via the seafront promenade&lt;/a&gt;, or embark on much longer excursions that can exceed 100 km, like those extending into the Montseny region.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Canet de Mar?
A: While many routes involve road cycling on shared roads, the easier coastal loops and promenades can be more suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with road riding. Always check the specific route details for traffic levels and terrain to ensure it meets your family's needs.
tips: Beautiful area to see the sea

Very nice road among the vegetation with very few cars.

Canet de Mar is located between Sant Pol and Arenys de Mar.

Apart from tourism, the area also lives from the cultivation of strawberries and flowers.

The historic center has several interesting modernist buildings.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: While the Komoot tour starts near Canet de Mar, specific parking information for this exact loop isn't detailed. However, Canet de Mar is a good starting point, and you can typically find parking in or around the town. Look for public parking areas or street parking that allows for longer durations, especially if you plan to complete the entire loop.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and difficulty like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as easy on Komoot, covering 24.15 km with 278 meters of elevation gain. However, local research suggests that similar routes in the area, including sections of this loop, are often considered moderate for road cycling due to rolling hills and varying inclines. You can expect mostly well-paved surfaces, but be aware that some moderate routes in the region might have short unpaved segments. The route offers a diverse experience with both coastal stretches and climbs into the hills.

faqs.question: What scenic views and points of interest can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The loop offers a mix of picturesque coastal and inland scenery. You'll cycle past beautiful beaches and cliffs, particularly around Sant Pol de Mar Cliffs and Beach and Canet de Mar Beach. The route also takes you through the charming town of Sant Pol de Mar. Inland, especially on the Arenys de Munt to Sant Cebrià de Vallalta Road segment, you'll find lush vegetation and forest, offering a verdant contrast to the sea views.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 24.15 km racebike loop is approximately 1 hour and 15 minutes (4496 seconds). This is an average estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or take breaks.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: While Komoot rates this specific loop as 'easy', the presence of rolling hills and an elevation gain of 278 meters means it's not entirely flat. If you are a complete beginner to road cycling, you might find some sections challenging. Those who prefer flatter rides or are less experienced with climbs might consider using an e-bike to make the experience more enjoyable.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Maresme area, where this loop is located, is known for excellent cycling conditions. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summer can be quite warm, especially midday, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter cycling is also possible, as the coastal climate is relatively mild, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially more variable weather.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Canet de Mar and Sant Pol de Mar, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stop for refreshments, snacks, or a meal. Sant Pol de Mar, in particular, is a charming fishing town with options for a break.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official paths. Notable sections include parts of the Eix de la Costa, Sant Cebrià de Vallalta-Arenys de Mar, and the Eix Central del Maresme. You'll also briefly encounter sections of El Camí IT7TR2 El Camí de Llevant: de Montserrat a Salses and Pineda de Mar-Sant Pol de Mar.

faqs.question: Are there any quiet sections with less car traffic?
faqs.answer: Yes, the segment from Arenys de Munt to Sant Cebrià de Vallalta Road is highlighted as a very nice road that winds among vegetation with very few cars. This section provides a more serene cycling experience away from busier coastal roads.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Canet de Mar - Sant Pol de Mar - Arenys de Munt Loop. It utilizes public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Is the Canet de Mar - Sant Pol de Mar - Arenys de Munt Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, it is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs, especially on leash, due to traffic and speed. If you wish to bring a dog, it would be best to transport them in a suitable bike trailer, but always prioritize their safety and comfort on public roads.
